payload.summary: Amendment fr-7-6ba24ae1 proposes a documentation comment to clarify the json_fill_none invariant. The code already has comments at both call sites explaining the rationale, but they don't explicitly warn about the conflict-semantics trap. The existing comments say "adding a new optional field automatically includes it in the merge" — which is true for non-conflicting fields but misleading for fields that need conflict detection. A targeted warning comment is a low-cost, high-value safeguard. Amendment fr-7-0cb31894 identifies a real gap in plan_hash backfill for existing rows during completion, but the code at lines 3231-3264 contains an explicit NOTE comment explaining this is intentional: "snapshot-based plan_hash backfill is intentionally NOT applied when finalizing an existing row. persist_plan() can advance snapshot.plan_hash after the attempt started, so the current snapshot hash may not match the plan the bead ran under." This is a deliberate design decision, not an oversight — backfilling from the snapshot during completion could assign the wrong plan_hash if the plan changed mid-flight.

votes.rationale: The existing comments at the json_fill_none call sites (lines ~2322 and ~2365) say 'adding a new optional field automatically includes it in the merge without manual enumeration' — which is correct for fill-only semantics but actively misleading for fields that need conflict detection. A developer adding a new optional field with conflict semantics (like run_id or plan_hash) could read that comment and conclude no additional work is needed, when in fact they must add an explicit option_conflicts() check above. Adding a targeted invariant comment costs nothing and prevents a subtle silent-data-loss bug class. This is a well-scoped, actionable documentation improvement.

The code at lines 3231-3264 already contains an explicit intentional-design comment: 'snapshot-based plan_hash backfill is intentionally NOT applied when finalizing an existing row. persist_plan() can advance snapshot.plan_hash after the attempt started, so the current snapshot hash may not match the plan the bead ran under. Only new entry creation auto-populates.' This is a deliberate correctness decision, not a bug. If the plan was persisted (advanced) after the bead started running, the current snapshot plan_hash reflects the NEW plan, not the plan the bead actually ran under. Backfilling from the snapshot during completion would assign incorrect lineage — the opposite of the stated goal. The 'reproduction' described (pre-seeding plan_hash=None, then completing with plan_hash=None while snapshot has a value) is the exact scenario the NOTE addresses: the system correctly refuses to guess which plan version the bead ran under when neither the start record nor the caller provides it. The caller (service.rs:557-563) forwarding plan_hash as-is is also correct — it's the caller's responsibility to provide the plan_hash the bead actually ran under, not the adapter's job to override it from a potentially-stale snapshot.
